reviewPurpose: Ask Glen to review whether the current Track 2 selected-node extraction can be mapped to TaPaaS schema concepts without overclaiming page status, schema compatibility or backend behaviour.

sourceNodes.safeLabel: Selected Figma node: likely Privacy acknowledgement normal state candidate
Selected Figma node: likely Privacy acknowledgement validation-error state candidate

pageStateMapSummary.languageToUse: 2 selected Figma nodes interpreted as likely Privacy acknowledgement normal/error states

candidateComponentMappings.evidence: Privacy collection notice and terms content detected in both selected nodes.
Terms acknowledgement label detected; validation message detected in 751:10322.
Header, navigation, logos, search, icons, footer and spacing instances detected.

validationEvidence: Accept the Terms and conditions to continue.

stateComparison.relationship: Likely normal/error state pair for a Privacy acknowledgement page/state candidate, subject to design owner confirmation.

figmaIntent: Whether the selected nodes are pages, component states or validation variants.
Whether frame order matches intended transaction order.
Whether Continue has an explicit target outside the selected evidence.
tapaasSchema: Real page, section, block, field, validation and CTA property names.
Which candidate block types match existing TaPaaS schema concepts.
Whether shell elements should be excluded, inherited or represented.
backend: Request fields for acknowledgement, consent or transaction progression.
Response fields for validation, next-page routing or error recovery.
Backend-owned error taxonomy and persistence/audit rules.
approval: GEL and TaPaaS approval are unknown.

asksForGlen: Confirm the minimal TaPaaS schema concepts for privacy notice, acknowledgement checkbox, validation error summary, field-level validation and CTA.
Confirm which detected shell elements should be ignored, inherited or represented in the schema.
Provide a minimal schema sample or block catalogue excerpt for this kind of acknowledgement step.
Confirm whether the generated pseudo-schema shape is useful as a review artefact, even though it is not a compatibility claim.

recommendedNextExperiment.summary: Compare these 2 selected nodes against Glen's minimal TaPaaS schema sample using a no-fetch run, then revise the Design IR and pseudo-schema candidate to separate page content, state differences, shell elements and validation rules.
successCriteria: No raw Figma JSON is sent.
The review pack keeps page/state candidate language.
Every TaPaaS mapping remains candidate/inferred/unknown unless directly confirmed.
Glen can identify which candidate fields are useful or wrong.
